teams.title: Oil Shock: How Iran's Rivalries Move Global Energy Markets
teams.recommended_idea: Oil Shock: How Iran's Rivalries Move Global Energy Markets
teams.submitted_idea: Oil Shock: How Iran's Rivalries Move Global Energy Markets
teams.alignment_note: Adopted the recommended idea directly. Focused on Strait of Hormuz dynamics and historical price spikes.
datasets_cited: World Bank Global Fuel Prices Database (1990-2024, 170+ countries)
US EIA International Energy Data (crude oil, production, trade flows, Hormuz throughput)
Manual geopolitical event annotations from EIA and news archives
strengths: Two authoritative, open datasets (EIA and World Bank) with clear links
Four quantified 'aha' insights including supply shock disparities across income levels
SQL query logic demonstrates genuine data engagement
Timely topic: Feb 2026 Brent crude above $100/barrel
concerns: Submission is relatively short (716 words) for a 4-person team
Oil/geopolitics is well-trodden territory; needs sharper differentiation
SQL code snippet feels somewhat templated
action_items: Add household energy cost impact layer to differentiate from generic oil analysis
Lock a data snapshot date to manage live-data risk
Expand narrative beyond price spikes to show who suffers most (Sub-Saharan Africa angle is strong)
Create a clear data freeze strategy for the remaining 2.5 weeks

teams.title: Is Dublin the Most Stressed City in Ireland?
teams.recommended_idea: Is Dublin the Most Stressed City in Ireland?
teams.submitted_idea: Is Dublin the Most Stressed City in Ireland?
teams.alignment_note: Adopted the recommended idea with a robust multi-indicator composite stress index approach.
datasets_cited: CSO Well-being Survey (SILC): life satisfaction, depression, loneliness, financial stress
Census Commuting Data: average journey times, long commutes by county
Census Housing and Rent Data: average weekly rent by county
RTB Rent Index: standardised monthly rents for new/existing tenancies
Census Population Statistics: population density and demographics
strengths: Five complementary CSO datasets, all open and well-documented
Six numbered 'aha' insights with specific statistics (e.g. Dublin rent 65-90% higher than Cork)
Data quality assessment section included (completeness, missing values, consistency)
Transparent composite index methodology
concerns: Proxy indicators for stress need careful methodological justification
Decision-maker audience (city planners, health officials) is clear but broad
Risk of cherry-picking indicators to confirm the premise
action_items: Document and justify each proxy indicator's inclusion in the stress index
Compare Dublin against at least 3 other Irish cities using the same composite
Frame as genuine inquiry, not foregone conclusion
Build reproducible Python pipeline for composite scoring

teams.title: Hidden Bottlenecks in Ireland's Hospital Waiting Lists
teams.submitted_idea: Hidden Bottlenecks in Ireland's Hospital Waiting Lists
teams.alignment_note: Switched from Housing Affordability to Healthcare Waiting Lists as advised. Focused on specialty-level bottleneck analysis with a strong KPI framework.
datasets_cited: National Treatment Purchase Fund (NTPF) Waiting List Data: specialty, volumes, time bands (0-6, 6-12, 12-18, 18+ months)
strengths: Most comprehensive submission (1,548 words) with 9 KPIs organised in three narrative Acts
Switched from Housing to Healthcare as advised, showing responsiveness to feedback
Novel 'Wait Pressure' ratio metric (patients >12mo / total patients)
Python/Google Colab analysis with four figures
concerns: Overlaps with Team 07 on hospital waiting lists; must differentiate angle
Single data source (NTPF) limits triangulation opportunities
Risk of analysis becoming too technical for non-specialist audience
action_items: Differentiate clearly from Team 07: focus on bottleneck mechanics, not budget allocation
Add Slaintecare time targets as benchmark lines on visualisations
Simplify KPI language for policy audience (avoid jargon like 'Wait Pressure')
Consider adding one complementary dataset (e.g. HSE funding by specialty)

teams.title: The Global Rise of Internet Shutdowns
teams.recommended_idea: The Global Rise of Internet Shutdowns
teams.submitted_idea: The Global Rise of Internet Shutdowns
teams.alignment_note: Adopted the recommended idea. Identified Access Now STOP dataset as primary source with economic cost dimension.
datasets_cited: Access Now #KeepItOn Shutdown Tracker (STOP): event-level, dates, countries, scope, type, reasons
Freedom House Freedom on the Net reports
NetBlocks Cost of Shutdown Tool (COST)
World Bank economic impact indicators
Top10VPN Global Cost of Internet Shutdowns 2024 report
strengths: Highly distinctive topic: digital rights and internet governance rarely visualised
Multiple complementary data sources including economic cost dimension
Clear preliminary insights: 2024 as record year, power concentrated in few states
Well-scoped next steps for data cleaning and narrative testing
concerns: Analytical depth is moderate: needs more specific statistics from the data
Decision-maker audience mentioned but not clearly specified
Submission length (846 words) could be more detailed for a 4-person team
action_items: Download and explore the STOP dataset: cite specific counts, trends, top countries
Define a specific decision-maker (e.g. UN Special Rapporteur on Freedom of Expression)
Lead with the trend line: 'Shutdowns increased X% over Y years'
Include 'cost of silence' metric per shutdown event

teams.title: The Invisible Pandemic: Antibiotic Resistance Funding
teams.submitted_idea: The Invisible Pandemic: Antibiotic Resistance Funding
teams.alignment_note: Followed the recommended topic but shifted from mortality/clinical angle to research funding analysis. Valid pivot but the original recommendation emphasised the mortality comparison hook.
datasets_cited: Global research funding dataset for bacterial infectious diseases (13,000+ projects)
Dataset fields: Project ID, Organisation, PI, Country, Dates, Investment (USD), Research area, Infectious agent
strengths: Funding angle provides a distinctive take on AMR that avoids clinical complexity
13,000+ project dataset enables meaningful aggregation and comparison
Geographic concentration finding (USA, UK dominate) is policy-relevant
Vaccines-vs-therapeutics funding gap is a clear, communicable insight
concerns: Lost the powerful mortality comparison hook (AMR kills more than HIV/malaria)
Dataset source and accessibility not clearly documented (no URLs)
Insights are somewhat generic: funding concentrated in rich countries is expected
action_items: Re-integrate the mortality comparison as the opening hook, then pivot to funding
Provide dataset URL and verify open access
Quantify specific funding gaps: how much per death for AMR vs HIV vs malaria?
Identify a specific decision-maker (e.g. WHO Director of AMR Coordination)
Add temporal trends: is AMR funding growing or stagnating?

teams.title: Flooded and Forgotten: Flood Risk and Deprivation in Ireland
teams.submitted_idea: Flooded and Forgotten: Flood Risk and Socioeconomic Deprivation in Ireland
teams.alignment_note: Adopted the recommended idea with impressive analytical rigour. Strongest correlation analysis in the cohort (r = -0.76).
datasets_cited: OPW Flood Zone Classifications (data.gov.ie): Zone A (>1% AEP), Zone B (0.1-1% AEP)
Pobal HP Deprivation Index: 209 Electoral Divisions, 33 counties, 8 NUTS3 regions
18 variables including deprivation scores, unemployment, education, lone-parent rates, flood zones
strengths: Most analytically rigorous submission: Pearson r = -0.76 (more deprivation = more vulnerability)
Five figures with clear methodology (scatter plots, bar charts, box plots, regional maps)
Policy gap identified: 7 of 31 Critically Vulnerable EDs lack flood relief schemes
Highly original topic: flood risk as social justice issue, not hydrology
concerns: Only 2 team members: capacity risk for remaining 2.5 weeks
Spatial data joining is technically demanding (Electoral Division level)
Decision-maker could be more specifically named (e.g. OPW Chief Engineer)
action_items: Prioritise ruthlessly given 2-person capacity: focus on 3-4 key visualisations
Name the 7 unprotected EDs explicitly as the actionable finding
Build narrative flow: start with one real flooded community, then zoom out
Simplify spatial approach: Electoral Division level, not property level

teams.title: Ireland's Waiting List Crisis: Where EUR 420M Is Not Going
teams.recommended_idea: Who Really Waits Longest in Ireland's Hospital Queues?
teams.submitted_idea: Ireland's Waiting List Crisis: Where EUR 420M Is Not Going
teams.alignment_note: Adopted the recommended waiting list topic with a distinctive budget accountability angle. EUR 420M framing and Pareto analysis add clear differentiation.
datasets_cited: NTPF Open Data: Outpatient waiting list (2,247 rows x 10 columns, Jan 2025-Feb 2026)
Government Waiting Time Action Plan 2025 (EUR 420M budget)
RTE and NTPF press releases
strengths: Powerful framing: EUR 420M spent yet extreme waits grew 10%
Pareto analysis: 5 specialties account for 57% of extreme wait backlog
Insight that chronic conditions (Dermatology, Endocrinology) queue longer because of lower political priority
Four figures with clear policy implications
concerns: Overlaps with Team 03 on hospital waiting lists; differentiation needed
Budget accountability angle could be strengthened with actual spend-per-specialty data
Single primary data source (NTPF)
action_items: Differentiate from Team 03: lead with budget allocation mismatch, not bottleneck mechanics
Source HSE or Department of Health spend-per-specialty data to complete the argument
Add Slaintecare targets as benchmark lines
Quantify the cost-per-patient-cleared for each specialty
Create a 'money follows the problem' scenario showing reallocation impact

teams.title: Geopolitical Conflict and Europe's Energy Transition
teams.recommended_idea: Europe Energy Risk from Strait of Hormuz Conflict
teams.submitted_idea: Geopolitical Conflict and Europe's Energy Transition
teams.alignment_note: Departed from the recommended Hormuz energy vulnerability angle. Now exploring whether Ukraine-related conflict accelerated Europe's renewable energy adoption using ACLED and Eurostat.
datasets_cited: ACLED Europe and Central Asia Conflict Dataset (~117,359 conflict events)
Eurostat Renewable Energy Statistics (2016-2024, share of renewables by country)
strengths: Two authoritative datasets (ACLED, Eurostat) with clear methodology
Pre/post war comparison: renewables share rose from ~19.9% to ~24.3%
Distinctive angle: conflict as accelerant of energy transition
Well-structured, methodical analysis
concerns: Departed from recommended Hormuz angle without explaining rationale
Correlation between conflict and renewable adoption does not establish causation
ACLED dataset is very large (117K events); needs aggressive filtering
action_items: Acknowledge the pivot from Hormuz and explain why the new angle is stronger
Focus on 5-6 specific European countries for tractable analysis
Be explicit about correlation vs causation limitations
Identify a specific decision-maker (e.g. EU Commissioner for Energy)
Add a counterfactual: what was the renewable trajectory before the war?

teams.title: Irish Road Safety: A Nation Moving in the Wrong Direction
teams.recommended_idea: Road Accident Analysis: Identifying High-Risk Conditions and Locations
teams.submitted_idea: Irish Road Safety: A Nation Moving in the Wrong Direction
teams.alignment_note: Adopted the recommended topic and improved it by switching from UK to Irish data (as advised). Best decision-maker persona in the cohort: RSA Director of Road Safety Strategy.
datasets_cited: ROA17 (Traffic Collisions & Casualties Monthly): RSA/CSO, 2005-2023
ROA20 (Traffic Collisions & Casualties by County): RSA/CSO, 2005-2012
RSA Provisional Fatality Statistics 2021-2026 (published March 2026)
EU Country Profile for Ireland (European Commission DG MOVE, Feb 2024)
ETSC 19th Road Safety Performance Index (PIN) Report (June 2025)
strengths: Five authoritative data sources across Irish and EU levels
Strongest decision-maker alignment: RSA Director of Road Safety Strategy
Counter-trend finding: Ireland +28% fatalities while EU -12.3%
Three specific, evidence-based policy recommendations with implementation pathways
concerns: Road safety dashboards are a well-established genre; needs visual distinctiveness
ROA20 dataset only runs to 2012; county-level data has a gap to present
action_items: Design a visually distinctive front page that passes the '5-second test'
Address the ROA20 data gap (2012-present) for county-level analysis
Lead with the counter-trend: Ireland going wrong while EU improves
Prototype the dashboard layout from the submission sketch

teams.title: The Global Wheat Shock: How the Ukraine War Disrupted Food Security
teams.recommended_idea: Global Food Prices and Cost of Living Crisis
teams.submitted_idea: The Global Wheat Shock: How the Ukraine War Disrupted Food Security
teams.alignment_note: Major improvement. Narrowed from generic food prices to Ukraine wheat supply disruption. Specific commodity, specific shock, specific victims. Exactly the kind of focusing the feedback recommended.
datasets_cited: FAO Food Price Monitoring & Analysis (FPMA): monthly wheat prices, Jan 2018-June 2024 (78 observations)
UN FAO Cereal Import Dependency Ratio (CIDR): 25 major nations (Item Code 21035)
FAO Trade Statistics: food CPI inflation for 17 countries (2021-2023)
strengths: Exceptional data exploration: pre/post invasion prices ($229 to $458.7/tonne = 58% spike)
Import dependency analysis identifies 6 countries with >50% dependency (Lebanon 81.4%)
Food inflation gap: high-dependency countries 167.4% vs low-dependency 11.3%
Clear decision-maker: World Food Programme, national food security regulators
concerns: Three FAO datasets need careful joining; some gaps likely in CPI data
Ukraine wheat narrative is well-covered in media; needs visual distinctiveness
action_items: Lead with the 156-percentage-point inflation gap as the headline finding
Build Lebanon as a sentinel case study (81.4% import dependency, 49.8% Ukrainian wheat)
Add a 'strategic reserve' or 'early warning' policy dimension
Ensure all three FAO datasets join cleanly on country identifiers

teams.title: The Loneliness Epidemic
teams.alignment_note: Adopted the recommended topic. Three datasets identified but no exploratory analysis performed. Submission is substantially below expectations for a 4-person team.
datasets_cited: OECD How's Life? Social Connections (47 countries): loneliness, social support, interactions
Meta-Gallup Global State of Social Connections (7 countries): nationally representative survey
Our World in Data: social support, one-person households, mortality linkage (100+ countries)
strengths: Three complementary datasets spanning different geographic scopes
Framing loneliness as physical health crisis (not just mental health) is powerful
Topic has genuine policy relevance and emotional resonance
concerns: No exploratory analysis performed: no figures, no insights, no statistics from data
Copy-paste error: 'We will draw on three complementary datasets' repeated verbatim
No target decision-maker identified
Very short submission (410 words) for a 4-person team
action_items: Immediately download and explore all three datasets
Verify Gallup data access (may require paid licence)
Define a specific decision-maker (e.g. Minister for Mental Health and Older People)
Lead with the mortality comparison: 'loneliness kills more than 15 cigarettes a day'
Produce at least 3 preliminary visualisations before the next milestone

teams.title: Conflict, Oil, and Economic Shockwaves
teams.recommended_idea: From Glitches to Global Risk: Mapping the Escalation of AI Incidents
teams.alignment_note: Ignored the recommended AI Incidents topic entirely. Reverted to Idea 1 (Oil/Conflict) which the feedback explicitly advised against as widely explored. No rationale provided for the departure.
datasets_cited: War Economic Impact Dataset (100,000 rows, 28 columns; source unspecified)
Oil Production by Country Dataset (17,992 rows, 4 columns; source unspecified)
GDP Growth Dataset (World Bank)
strengths: Basic descriptive statistics provided (avg GDP change -31.61% during conflicts)
Large dataset (100K rows) could enable meaningful analysis if properly explored
Topic has inherent public interest
concerns: Ignored lecturer recommendation without explanation
Shortest submission in cohort (253 words): reads as an outline, not a completed deliverable
No dataset URLs, no source provenance, no data quality assessment
No target decision-maker identified
action_items: Urgent: provide dataset URLs and verify data provenance
Define a specific decision-maker and key message
Perform genuine exploratory analysis with at least 3 visualisations
Consider returning to AI Incidents as recommended, or provide clear justification for this pivot
Expand submission to demonstrate team has engaged with the data
